audience.description: New programmers and working developers who need to build, test, secure, diagnose, and defend current Go programs from first execution through production-shaped concurrent services.
entryKnowledge: Navigate files, edit text, use a browser, and follow exact punctuation and indentation.
Reason with basic arithmetic, comparisons, plain-language conditions, and step-by-step procedures; prior programming is helpful but not required.
deviceConstraints: Core language labs execute locally in browser WebAssembly through a restricted Yaegi interpreter; a physical keyboard is recommended for projects.
Toolchain, filesystem, test-command, network, race-detector, profiler, and deployment work uses deterministic evidence simulations and explicit real-toolchain transfer instructions.
accessibilityAssumptions: All code, traces, channel timelines, profiles, request flows, tables, and diagnostics require structured text, keyboard operation, announced status, and non-color-only meaning.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Navigate files, edit text, use a browser, and follow exact punctuation and indentation.

A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Reason with basic arithmetic, comparisons, plain-language conditions, and step-by-step procedures; prior programming is helpful but not required.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 5 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: Go language specification through Go 1.26, the Go 1 compatibility model, current toolchain behavior, packages, modules, workspaces, testing, and standard-library design

Cumulative runnable work across values, control flow, functions, collections, Unicode, structs, methods, interfaces, errors, generics, goroutines, channels, context, synchronization, I/O, JSON, and service boundaries
Production practice covering fuzzing, race evidence, security, vulnerability management, HTTP lifecycles, diagnostics, profiling, PGO, release gates, and graceful operations
excludes: Treating the browser Yaegi subset as proof of full Go 1.26 toolchain compatibility, host access, race-detector behavior, or production performance
Framework-specific certification, kernel implementation, unsafe programming, cgo internals, and exhaustive standard-library API memorization

sources.title: ACM/IEEE-CS/AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023
sources.scope: Controls institutional software-development breadth, programming-language foundations, algorithms, testing, professional practice, security, systems, and concurrency outcomes.

sources.title: The Go Programming Language Specification
sources.scope: Controls lexical, type, declaration, expression, statement, function, method, interface, generic, initialization, and program semantics.

sources.title: Go 1.26 Release Notes and Release History
sources.scope: Controls current language changes, toolchain modernizers, module defaults, Green Tea GC, standard-library changes, compatibility, and supported-platform boundaries.

sources.title: Go Modules Reference
sources.scope: Controls module, package, version, minimal-version-selection, checksum, proxy, private-module, workspace, and toolchain-directive behavior.

sources.title: The Go Memory Model and Data Race Detector
sources.version: Memory model 2022-06-06; race detector current for Go 1.26
sources.scope: Controls data-race definitions, happens-before reasoning, channel and lock synchronization, atomic publication, detector evidence, and limitations.

sources.title: Go Testing, Fuzzing, and Command Documentation
sources.scope: Controls unit tests, subtests, examples, fuzz targets and corpora, benchmarks, coverage, test caching, and changed-case quality evidence.

sources.title: Go Concurrency Patterns: Context, Pipelines, and Cancellation
sources.scope: Controls pipeline ownership, explicit cancellation, context propagation, deadlines, goroutine termination, fan-out, fan-in, and leak prevention.

sources.title: Security Best Practices for Go Developers
sources.scope: Controls patched toolchains, dependency review, govulncheck, fuzzing, race checks, input limits, secrets, and secure release practice.

sources.title: Go Diagnostics and Profile-Guided Optimization
sources.scope: Controls selection and interpretation of profiles, traces, runtime evidence, debugging, production diagnostics, and PGO experiments.

sources.title: Go Code Review Comments and Doc Comments
sources.scope: Controls idiomatic API naming, package design, receiver choices, error style, documentation, readability, and maintainable review decisions.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.

competencies.statement: Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.
misconceptions: Using a memory-safe language removes injection, authorization, resource-exhaustion, and logic risks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.

competencies.statement: Map internal failures to stable external responses while retaining structured correlation, cause, latency, and privacy-safe logs.
misconceptions: Returning the full internal error text gives clients the most useful and secure diagnosis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map internal failures to stable external responses while retaining structured correlation, cause, latency, and privacy-safe logs.

competencies.statement: Coordinate signal handling, readiness withdrawal, server shutdown, in-flight deadlines, background-worker completion, and forced-exit evidence.
misconceptions: Calling Server.Close immediately is equivalent to graceful Shutdown.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Coordinate signal handling, readiness withdrawal, server shutdown, in-flight deadlines, background-worker completion, and forced-exit evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain stack and heap escape, allocation, garbage-collection cost, Go 1.26 Green Tea behavior, and measurement limits without manual-memory myths.
misconceptions: Every pointer allocates on the heap and every allocation should be eliminated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain stack and heap escape, allocation, garbage-collection cost, Go 1.26 Green Tea behavior, and measurement limits without manual-memory myths.

competencies.statement: Choose CPU, heap, block, mutex, goroutine, execution trace, metrics, logs, or debugger evidence for the observed question.
misconceptions: A CPU profile is the correct first tool for every latency or memory problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose CPU, heap, block, mutex, goroutine, execution trace, metrics, logs, or debugger evidence for the observed question.

competencies.statement: Benchmark a representative baseline, form a causal hypothesis, inspect profiles, change one factor, validate behavior, and evaluate PGO portability.
misconceptions: A microbenchmark improvement guarantees lower production latency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Benchmark a representative baseline, form a causal hypothesis, inspect profiles, change one factor, validate behavior, and evaluate PGO portability.

competencies.statement: Gate current toolchain patches, tests, race and fuzz evidence, govulncheck reachability, dependency provenance, reproducible builds, and rollback.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate current toolchain patches, tests, race and fuzz evidence, govulncheck reachability, dependency provenance, reproducible builds, and rollback.
